html:
Global attention is gripped by the escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, with both nations exchanging strikes that threaten to derail a fragile ceasefire and halt critical talks. This comes as Iran prepares for the funeral of its martyred Leader, adding another layer of uncertainty to regional stability.

US-Iran Tensions: Escalating strikes between the US and Iran are challenging an interim peace agreement, with a Qatari citizen reported killed by shrapnel from military operations and planned talks in Switzerland stalling. President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, while Iran denounced a "flagrant violation" after US strikes and threatened to halt negotiations entirely, also canceling participation in technical talks. Amidst this, Iran is preparing for the funeral of its martyred Leader, and its Vice President announced foreign dignitaries would begin arriving for the event.

Russia-Ukraine Conflict: Ukraine has intensified its offensive, striking Russian weapons plants and fuel hubs in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, igniting a major oil refinery, and pummeling Crimea. These actions, which have led Russian-installed authorities to declare an economic emergency in Crimea, are part of President Zelenskyy's stated strategy to bring the war to the Russian people. Putin has acknowledged a "difficult period" and "certain shortages" of energy amidst Ukrainian attacks, while vowing to ensure Russia’s security. Meanwhile, the European Commission is considering withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males, a move supported by Kyiv and several EU countries.

Middle East Security: Beyond the US-Iran friction, violence continues in the broader Middle East, with Israeli forces killing four Palestinians in Gaza amidst ongoing attacks on tents and pushing deeper into southern Syria. A US-backed agreement between Lebanon and Israel has been announced, but it faces significant internal opposition, with Nabih Berri stating it "will not pass" and Naim Qassem declaring it "null and void." In Iraq, an anti-corruption campaign has led to the arrest of 47 officials, some reportedly tied to Iran-aligned parties. Saudi Arabia's state oil giant Aramco experienced a helicopter crash that killed all 14 Saudi nationals on board.

Global Climate & Disasters: Europe is grappling with a record-breaking heatwave linked to over 1,300 deaths, with Germany hitting 41.7C, prompting the WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus to warn of the continent's unpreparedness. A dangerous heatwave with high humidity is also forecast to blast a large swath of the central and eastern United States, impacting areas ahead of the Fourth of July holiday. Concurrently, three firefighters were killed and two injured battling huge wildfires raging along the Colorado-Utah border, leading both governors to declare states of emergency. Venezuela continues to search for survivors after twin earthquakes killed over 1,450 people, with rescue efforts ongoing despite fading hope.

US Domestic & Policy: Homeland Security secretary Markwayne Mullin urged migrants on temporary protected status to seek permanent residence or leave, following a controversial supreme court ruling that stripped humanitarian protections from hundreds of thousands. Separately, New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani stated that he and progressive allies who swept recent primaries are carrying a "national message" to working Americans. On the economic front, a new US trade probe is targeting Germany's drug industry, reigniting a long-running clash over medical innovation costs.

Other Global Developments: Uganda's military chief, who is also the president’s son, has ordered the shutdown of leading media outlets NTV and Daily Monitor, which report their offices are under "military siege." In Albania, protests dubbed the 'Flamingo Revolution' continue, with demonstrators fighting for democracy, which PM Edi Rama insists is part of a hybrid war and is tied to a Kushner-linked development. Elsewhere, an Australian man has been charged with murder after a dead girl was found in a suitcase in Thailand, while a British man was arrested in Ecuador in connection with a woman's body found in a suitcase in Colombia.
